creator: mshirk@daemon-security.com
text:
Tested on recent CURRENT build (10/29/2017 19:14 EST)
                          
The way to reproduce is to first setup a zpool on a USB 3.0 key. With xorg running (had iridium and conky with i3 running and Xorg required processes), start a build world on the system (with -j8) and try to import the zpool.

This problem has been around for a while, but normally when I mention "USB", it seems to be an expected problem. However, with a debugging kernel I was able to get a solaris assertion in the debugging output:


solaris assert: refcount_count(&spa->spa_refcount) >= spa->spa_minref || MUTEX_HELD(&spa_namespace_lock), file: /usr/src/sys/cddl/contrib/opensolaris/uts/common/fs/zfs/spa_misc.c, line: 906
